label: Economic conversion factors for EU Cohesion Policy CBA

standard_conversion_factor.description: Converts market prices to economic (shadow) prices by adjusting for trade distortions (tariffs, subsidies, indirect taxes). For EU member states with low trade distortions, SCF is close to 1.0.
standard_conversion_factor.note: For most EU member states, SCF = 0.96-1.00. For IPA/candidate countries with higher trade barriers, SCF may be lower (0.85-0.95).

shadow_wage_rate.description: The economic opportunity cost of labour, adjusted for unemployment and tax wedges. Lower than market wage in high-unemployment regions (labour has lower opportunity cost).
shadow_wage_rate.formula: SWR = market_wage * (1 - u) * (1 - t) + reservation_wage * u

components.t: Tax and social contribution wedge (typically 0.30-0.45 in EU)
components.reservation_wage: Minimum income level (social benefits, subsistence). Typically 40-70% of market wage.

low_unemployment.label: Shadow wage rate factor when regional unemployment < 5%

medium_unemployment.label: Shadow wage rate factor when regional unemployment 5-15%

high_unemployment.label: Shadow wage rate factor when regional unemployment > 15%

shadow_wage_rate.note: SWRF = shadow wage / market wage. Multiply market labour costs by SWRF to get economic cost of labour.

sector_conversion_factors.description: Sector-specific factors for converting financial costs to economic costs.

construction.note: Construction costs often include indirect taxes and monopoly rents. Factor < 1.0.

energy.note: Energy prices include significant taxes and levies. Economic cost is lower than financial cost.

land.note: Land has no economic cost in CBA (transfer payment), but may have opportunity cost. Use agricultural output value or development value foregone.

source.publication: Guide to Cost-Benefit Analysis of Investment Projects for Cohesion Policy 2014-2020, Chapter 2
source.publisher: European Commission, DG Regional and Urban Policy

caveats: Shadow pricing adds complexity. For most EU CBAs, the adjustment from market to economic prices is small (within 5-10%).
Country-specific conversion factors should be calculated based on national trade statistics and tax structures.
These reference values are starting points. JASPERS or national appraisal bodies may publish country-specific factors.
